text.content:
First archetype outside the 2-on-1 family. Tests whether the prompt template structure scales beyond Decision-Making archetypes into Spatial Awareness territory.

Design philosophy for U7 content:
- Camera height dropped to 3'6" (vs 4'6" for U11) to match actual player perspective
- Stylized photorealism rather than full photorealism (AI tools can be cautious with very young children)
- Bright, inviting palette (not the cinematic moody style of older POV images)
- Lesson is recognition, not analysis: 'see open ice, skate forward'

Read Trigger Design Rule applied: this is an absence-based read (no defender, no decision pressure), so composition carries the lesson — wide open expanse of ice dominates the frame. Same principle as variant C of the 2-on-1 (defender splits the difference).

Next: generate 5+ variants in Gemini or DALL-E. Test read clarity with kid — should hear 'skate fast,' 'go,' 'take it up the ice.' If kid says 'pass' or 'shoot,' image rendered wrong.

Future variants of this archetype to consider:
- IMG-oic-002: Open ice in OZ after zone entry (slightly more advanced — 'now what?')
- IMG-oic-003: Open ice in DZ on a breakout (read: skate it out, don't dump)
- IMG-oic-004: Open ice during a rush, alone (read: build speed, attack)

text.content:
Generate a photorealistic first-person POV image from a young youth hockey player's perspective, skating with the puck in open ice with no defenders nearby. The player is approximately 5 years old (U7 / Initiation level).

POINT OF VIEW:
- Eye-level view approximately 3'6" above the ice (very young player height — this is critical, the camera is LOW)
- The viewer is the puck carrier, mid-stride forward
- The viewer's own gloved hands are visible in the lower foreground gripping a small youth-sized hockey stick
- A black hockey puck rests flat on the ice directly under the stick blade
- WHITE jersey sleeves visible at the bottom edges of the frame
- Because the camera is so low, the stick and gloves loom larger in the frame than they would for an older player

LOCATION ON ICE:
- The viewer is in the NEUTRAL ZONE, approximately mid-ice
- The center red line is visible crossing the lower-mid frame
- The far blue line (offensive zone entry) is visible in the upper third of the frame
- White polished ice fills MOST of the frame — a wide, open expanse of clean ice ahead of the viewer
- The image should feel SPACIOUS and INVITING, with lots of empty white ice
- Boards visible at the far edges of the frame (left and right) with white dasher and yellow kickplate
- Plexiglass with subtle reflections in the background

PLAYERS IN FRAME (zero other players — the viewer is alone with the puck):
- NO defenders
- NO teammates
- NO goalie visible
- NO other skaters anywhere in the frame
- Just the viewer's POV (gloves, stick, puck) and wide open ice

THE READ (this is what a U7 player should see):
- A wide-open expanse of empty ice ahead
- Nothing in the way, nothing to think about
- The puck on their stick, ready to go
- The image should make it visually obvious that the right move is to SKATE FORWARD with the puck

STYLE:
- Photorealistic youth sports photography style, with a slightly warm, inviting color palette appropriate for content aimed at young children
- Indoor minor hockey arena setting (community rink, NOT a professional NHL arena)
- Bright, clean, well-lit composition — NOT moody or dramatic lighting
- Cinematic composition, shallow depth of field
- Slight motion blur on skates suggesting forward movement
- 35mm lens look, documentary style
- Sharp focus on the puck and the open ice
- Crisp, friendly arena atmosphere
- The mood is 'go for it, the ice is yours'

DO NOT INCLUDE:
- Adult or professional-sized players (the viewer is age 5-6)
- Jersey numbers, team logos, or any text
- ANY other players in the frame (no defenders, no teammates, no goalie, no other skaters at all)
- A goal or net (this is the neutral zone, far from any goal)
- NHL-style professional arena (this is a youth community rink)
- Multiple pucks
- Distorted or warped hockey sticks
- Visible faces (helmet and cage should obscure any visible parts)
- Extra fingers or warped hands
- Crowd or spectators in sharp focus
- Dark, dramatic, or moody lighting (this is for young children — keep it bright and inviting)
- A cluttered or busy frame (open ice is the entire point)

Aspect ratio: 16:9 widescreen

text.content: IMG-oic-001

Generate a photorealistic first-person POV image from a youth hockey player's perspective defending in their own zone, with the stick flat on the ice cutting off a passing lane. All players in this image are approximately 8 to 11 years old (youth/minor hockey age).

POINT OF VIEW:
- Eye-level view approximately 4'6" above the ice (slight knee bend)
- The viewer is a defensive player in their own zone
- The viewer's own gloved hands are visible low in the foreground gripping a youth-sized hockey stick
- The stick blade is FLAT ON THE ICE and angled diagonally across the frame to physically block a passing lane
- BLACK jersey sleeves visible at the bottom edges of the frame (POV team is always BLACK)

LOCATION ON ICE:
- The viewer is in their defensive zone
- The blue line is visible in the upper half of the frame
- Boards on the right side of the frame
- White polished ice, professional overhead arena lighting

KEY VISUAL — stick lying flat across the passing lane (most important):
- The stick blade is flat on the ice, angled diagonally across the frame
- The blade visibly fills the LINE between the opposing puck carrier on the wall and the open opposing teammate in the slot
- The blade is the SHADOW that takes the pass away

PLAYERS IN FRAME (exactly 2 opposing players plus the POV's own goalie and a teammate):
- OPPOSING PUCK CARRIER in a WHITE jersey on the right wall at mid-distance, with the puck on his stick
- OPEN OPPOSING TEAMMATE in a WHITE jersey in the slot at mid-distance, calling for a pass with stick on ice
- THE POV TEAM'S OWN GOALIE in the crease behind the viewer, wearing BLACK pads and a black jersey (matching the POV team — your goalie is always BLACK in defensive zone scenes)
- A SECOND TEAMMATE DEFENDER in a BLACK jersey on the far side

STYLE:
- Photorealistic youth sports photography
- Indoor minor hockey arena setting (community rink, not professional NHL arena)
- Cinematic composition, shallow depth of field
- 35mm lens look, documentary style
- Sharp focus on the stick blade and the passing lane it covers

DO NOT INCLUDE:
- Adult or professional-sized players (all players are youth, ages 8-11)
- Mismatched jersey colors (POV team and own goalie MUST both be black, opposing team MUST be white)
- Stick raised in the air, on the player's hip, or held vertical
- Jersey numbers, team logos, or any text
- NHL-style professional arena
- Distorted or warped hockey sticks, extra fingers, warped skates
- Visible faces (helmets and cages should obscure them)
- Crowd or spectators in sharp focus

Aspect ratio: 16:9 widescreen

text.content: IMG-stickoi-001

Generate a photorealistic first-person POV image from a youth hockey player's perspective turning their back to a forechecker in the neutral zone to shield the puck. All players in this image are approximately 10 to 11 years old (youth/minor hockey age — U11).

POINT OF VIEW:
- Eye-level view approximately 4'8" above the ice
- The viewer is the puck carrier in the neutral zone, body TURNED so the camera looks back over the right shoulder
- The viewer's own gloved hands are visible low in the foreground gripping a youth-sized hockey stick
- The puck is tucked on the ice BEHIND the protected right hip
- The stick blade is flat on the ice next to the puck
- BLACK jersey sleeves visible at the bottom edges of the frame (POV team is always BLACK)
- The viewer's RIGHT shoulder is pressed outward toward the on-coming defender

LOCATION ON ICE:
- The viewer is in the neutral zone
- The center red line is visible in mid-frame
- White polished ice, professional overhead arena lighting

KEY VISUAL — body turned away from pressure (most important):
- The camera angle clearly shows the viewer has TURNED rather than skating forward into the forechecker
- The puck sits in a visible "safe pocket" behind the viewer's body
- The white-jersey forechecker is closing from the FRONT (toward the viewer's front shoulder), but the body is angled so his stick can't reach the puck

PLAYERS IN FRAME (exactly 2 youth players):
- ON-COMING FORECHECKER in a WHITE jersey, closing from the viewer's front, gloves and stick visible at mid-distance
- SUPPORTING TEAMMATE in a BLACK jersey on the left side of the frame, curling up ice into a passing lane (this is who the viewer should pass to once they've bought time)

STYLE:
- Photorealistic youth sports photography
- Indoor minor hockey arena setting (community rink, not professional NHL arena)
- Cinematic composition, shallow depth of field
- 35mm lens look, documentary style
- Sharp focus on the puck position behind the hip

DO NOT INCLUDE:
- Adult or professional-sized players (all players are youth, ages 10-11)
- Mismatched jersey colors (POV team and teammate MUST be black, forechecker MUST be white)
- Body squared up to the defender (must be turned, back/shoulder toward the pressure)
- Puck on the wrong (exposed) side of the body
- Jersey numbers, team logos, or any text
- NHL-style professional arena
- Distorted or warped hockey sticks, extra fingers, warped skates
- Visible faces (helmets and cages should obscure them)
- Crowd or spectators in sharp focus

Aspect ratio: 16:9 widescreen

text.content: IMG-pp-002

Generate a photorealistic first-person POV image from a youth hockey player's perspective receiving a pass with soft hands — the puck is mid-arrival on the stick blade. All players in this image are approximately 6 to 11 years old (youth/minor hockey age).

POINT OF VIEW:
- Eye-level view approximately 4'8" above the ice (youth player height)
- The viewer is an off-puck player about to receive a pass
- The viewer's own gloved hands are visible low in the foreground gripping a youth-sized hockey stick
- The stick is extended forward and the blade is FLAT ON THE ICE, slightly closed (cupped toward the player)
- The black puck is shown MID-ARRIVAL on the blade, half-on-half-touching
- The viewer's hands are SUBTLY pulled back about 6 inches as a soft-hands cushion (the cushion motion is the focal point)
- BLACK jersey sleeves visible at the bottom edges of the frame (POV team is always BLACK)

LOCATION ON ICE:
- The viewer is in the neutral zone
- The center red line is visible behind the viewer
- White polished ice, professional overhead arena lighting

KEY VISUAL — the cushion (most important):
- The blade is visibly CUPPED, not flat-open
- The hands are PULLING BACK rather than stabbing forward into the puck
- The puck is half-on-the-blade as it arrives, rendered with slight motion blur

PLAYERS IN FRAME (exactly 1 visible teammate stick + 1 distant defender):
- TEAMMATE PASSER's stick blade visible at the right edge of the frame, with a BLACK jersey cuff visible just above the glove (just released the pass)
- DEFENDER in a WHITE jersey two strides behind the receiver, visible at mid-distance but not yet pressing

STYLE:
- Photorealistic youth sports photography
- Indoor minor hockey arena setting (community rink, not professional NHL arena)
- Cinematic composition, shallow depth of field, slight motion blur on the puck arc
- 35mm lens look, documentary style
- Sharp focus on the stick blade and arriving puck

DO NOT INCLUDE:
- Adult or professional-sized players (all players are youth, ages 6-11)
- Mismatched jersey colors (POV team and teammate MUST be black, defender MUST be white)
- Stick blade lifted off the ice
- Blade open and rigid (blade must be cupped/closed)
- Puck bouncing off the blade
- Jersey numbers, team logos, or any text
- NHL-style professional arena
- Distorted or warped hockey sticks, extra fingers, warped skates
- Visible faces (helmets and cages should obscure them)
- Crowd or spectators in sharp focus

Aspect ratio: 16:9 widescreen
